data.content:
Plan: centralize provider runtime launch settings without changing agent labels.

Context found:
- Launch args are currently assembled in src/lib/agent-adapter.ts. Codex prompt mode and interactive app-server only pass model/approval settings today; Claude prompt mode and interactive launch only pass model/permission settings.
- Settings schema/defaults live in src/lib/schemas.ts and src/lib/settings-core.ts, with partial merge plumbing in src/lib/settings-types.ts and src/lib/settings-update.ts.
- Settings-backed agent targets are produced by resolveDispatchAgent, resolveOrchestrationAgent, getScopeRefinementAgent, stale-beat grooming helpers, and toCliTarget/agent-pool target construction.
- Local CLI sanity check: Codex accepts config overrides through repeated -c key=value and current config uses service_tier = "fast" plus model_reasoning_effort. Claude Code 2.1.158 exposes --effort with low, medium, high, xhigh, max, and exposes no speed flag.

Implementation steps:
1. Add central runtime settings to the settings schema, for example agentRuntime.codex and agentRuntime.claude. Defaults should be codex.speed = "fast", codex.reasoning = "high", and claude.reasoning = "high". Do not add a Claude speed field.
2. Validate provider-supported effort values at the schema boundary: Codex low/medium/high/xhigh; Claude low/medium/high/xhigh/max. Treat Codex speed as the Codex service tier value "fast" for now.
3. Extend SettingsPartial and mergeSettingsPartial so PATCH/PUT can update agentRuntime without clobbering other settings. Update docs/SETTINGS.md with the new section and examples.
4. Add a small runtime-settings helper, likely src/lib/agent-runtime-settings.ts, that resolves effective runtime settings for a target. It should use the central defaults when no settings were attached and should attach settings without touching identity fields or labels.
5. Thread settings-backed runtime config onto CliAgentTarget through dispatch resolution paths: resolveDispatchAgent for take/scene pools, resolveOrchestrationAgent for orchestration, getScopeRefinementAgent, and stale-beat grooming resolution. Prefer wrapping selected targets at these call sites so agent-pool.ts stays below the 500-line cap.
6. Update buildCodexInteractiveArgs and the Codex branch of buildPromptModeArgs to append -c service_tier="fast" when configured and -c model_reasoning_effort="<value>" for the configured/default effort. Keep existing model and approval behavior intact.
7. Update buildClaudeInteractiveArgs and buildClaudePromptModeArgs to append --effort <value> for the configured/default Claude effort. Do not append any speed-related Claude arg.
8. Keep normalizeRegisteredAgentConfig, formatAgentDisplayLabel, scan option labels, and AgentDisplayLabel behavior unchanged. Runtime settings must not be folded into label, provider, model, flavor, or version fields.
9. If file length gets tight, extract helper functions from agent-adapter.ts rather than pushing any source file over 500 lines.

Test strategy:
- Unit test settings defaults/load/merge to assert agentRuntime defaults and partial updates.
- Unit test invalid provider efforts are rejected by the schema.
- Unit test Codex prompt mode and app-server args include service_tier fast and model_reasoning_effort high by default, and pass through a configured xhigh value.
- Unit test Claude prompt mode and interactive args include --effort high by default and pass through max, while never containing speed/service_tier args.
- Add dispatch/target tests proving settings-backed selected agents receive runtime settings and labels stay identical to existing expectations.
- Run bun install --frozen-lockfile in the implementation worktree before gates if node_modules is missing, then bun run lint, bunx tsc --noEmit, bun run test, and bun run build.

Complexity/risk:
- Small-to-medium change, mostly schema/types/adapter/tests/docs.
- Main risk is failing to thread runtime settings to every settings-backed launch path, leaving some launches on defaults only.
- Secondary risk is Codex config key drift; tests should assert the concrete current CLI args and the implementation should keep the mapping isolated in one helper.
- No child knots needed.
